answer:
The significance level is the probability of a Type I error and equals the size of the test, so (i) is correct. For a two-sided test at a 5% significance level, the rejection region is split equally, with 2.5% in each tail, making (iii) correct and (iv) incorrect. The power of the test is not equal to the significance level, so (ii) is incorrect.  

\boxed{H}
